steps.title: Distribute and combine like terms
steps.content: Drop the parentheses and combine like terms:$$=9x^3+5x+7+6x^3+5x^2-5$$$$=15x^3+5x^2+5x+2$$

steps.title: Match to the answer choices
steps.content: The simplified expression is $15x^3+5x^2+5x+2$. That matches choice D — D is correct.

steps.title: Use the source rationale
steps.content: Choice D is correct. The given expression can be rewritten as $\left(9{x}^{3}+6{x}^{3}\right)+5{x}^{2}+5x+\left(7-5\right)$. Combining like terms in this expression yields $15{x}^{3}+5{x}^{2}+5x+2$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice A is incorrect and may result from conceptual or calculation errors.
